heading: Sixto topology witness extracts machine-readable primitives and labels from the five source assets before QA promotion
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, arto-heino, sixto-ramos, topology-witness, external-prior-art]
body: Built `sixto_topology_witness.json` and `build_sixto_topology_witness.py` from the actual Arto source asset set after downloading and inspecting the five Sixto images. The witness now records per-asset source metadata (attachment id, original file URL, original size, source line) plus extracted topology. Stable cross-asset invariants: a repeated two-hub geometry linked by a waist/crossing bundle; later unfolding into an H/N stage chain with K-distance labels; repeated phase/time indexing through X-labels and Z1/Z2; and an explicit Archimedean twin-circle overview with the number ladder 17, 34, 55, 89, 144. Honest verdict: this is the first real machine-readable Sixto witness, but it is still raster-level topology, not exact geometry. The next correct moves are to vectorize `CCH-Archm1b` and to transcribe the H1/N1..H4/N4 plus K1..K4 chain from `Sioxto3002B` before promoting any QA/RT law.
